title: read_message returns anyhow::Err for not-found, causing serverFail in message_set readAt path
description: store::read_message uses anyhow::bail!("message not found") when the message row is absent. In message_set, the readAt patch path calls read_message and maps any Err to serverFail. Because bail! is indistinguishable from a genuine DB error, a concurrent hard-delete between the pre-check and the readAt write produces serverFail instead of notFound. The store method should return Ok(false) for not-found and Err only for I/O failures.
